◎简　　介  

　　Artist Pamela Masik painted massive, brutal portraits of Vancouver’s missing and murdered women to condemn a culture whose prejudices contributed to their violent fate. But, in the horrific light of the Pickton trial, she is instead accused of exploitation.
introduction: Artist Pamela Masik painted massive, brutal portraits of Vancouver’s missing and murdered women to condemn a culture whose prejudices contributed to their violent fate. But, in the horrific light of the Pickton trial, she is instead accused of exploitation.
